Funder: Natural Sciences and Engineering Research Council of Canada (NSERC)
data_access_collaborators: Project data will be stored in a secure cloud-based repository (Ontario Trusted Research Environment) with role-based access. Collaborators will access and update data via authenticated APIs and version-controlled repositories (GitHub + internal data lake).
data_collection: The project will collect soil composition data, crop yield measurements, satellite imagery (NDVI), weather data, and farmer survey responses. External datasets from Environment Canada and FAO will also be integrated.
data_sharing_awareness: Datasets will be registered with DOI via Dataverse and indexed in OpenAIRE and Google Dataset Search. Publications and conference presentations will reference the datasets.
data_sharing_form: Processed and cleaned datasets will be shared, along with derived analytical datasets and final aggregated outputs. Raw data containing sensitive information will be restricted.
data_sharing_license: Open datasets will be released under CC BY 4.0. Sensitive or restricted datasets will require controlled access agreements.
documentation_collection: Documentation will be maintained using standardized templates in GitHub, including README files, data dictionaries, and changelogs enforced through CI workflows.
documentation_data: Documentation will include variable definitions, units, methodologies, data provenance, processing scripts, and metadata compliant with DCAT-AP and Dublin Core.
file_formats: Data will be collected in CSV, JSON, GeoTIFF, and Parquet formats. Open, non-proprietary formats will be prioritized to ensure long-term accessibility.
long_term_deposition: Final datasets will be deposited in Scholars Portal Dataverse and Zenodo for long-term preservation and public access.
manage_IP_issues: All contributors will sign data ownership agreements. Intellectual property will follow institutional policies, with licensing clearly defined for each dataset.
metadata_standards: DCAT-AP, Dublin Core, ISO 19115 for geospatial data, and schema.org Dataset markup
preservation_ready: Data will be converted to preservation-friendly formats (CSV, TIFF), checksums will be generated for integrity, sensitive data will be anonymized, and full metadata documentation will accompany all datasets.
project_abstract: This project investigates the relationship between soil health, climate variability, and crop yield optimization using integrated environmental and agricultural datasets across Ontario.
project_title: Integrated Soil and Climate Data for Sustainable Crop Yield Optimization

research_outputs.Description: Raw soil sensor and survey dataset including geolocated measurements.
research_outputs.Title: Ontario Soil Health Raw Dataset

research_outputs.Description: Interactive dashboard for visualizing crop yield predictions and environmental correlations.
research_outputs.Title: Agriculture Insights Dashboard

research_outputs.Description: Satellite-derived crop imagery dataset for vegetation analysis.
research_outputs.Title: Satellite Crop Imagery Dataset

resources_for_dmp: Resources include cloud storage (AWS S3), data management personnel, and compute resources for processing geospatial data. Estimated total cost: CAD $25,000.
responsibilities: The Data Manager oversees data organization, storage, and documentation. The PI ensures compliance and oversight. Analysts handle data processing and quality control.
responsibilities_change_management: In case of personnel changes, responsibilities will be transferred via documented handover procedures, including access credentials, documentation, and task tracking systems.
secondary_use_sensitive_data: Sensitive datasets will be shared under controlled access agreements with strict usage terms and ethical approval requirements.
secure_sensitive_data: Sensitive data will be encrypted at rest and in transit, stored in secure institutional environments, and accessed only via authenticated and authorized channels.
storage_how: Data will be stored in AWS S3 with automated backups and mirrored in institutional servers. Versioning will be enabled.
storage_requirements: Estimated 5 TB of storage over the project lifecycle, retained for a minimum of 10 years.
version_convention: Files will follow semantic versioning (v1.0, v1.1), with structured naming conventions including date, dataset type, and version. Git will be used for tracking changes.
